levels.title: Needle in a Haystack
levels.narrative: The phantom_admin's diary has hundreds of entries. Somewhere in it, they mention a 'password'. You need to search through the file without reading every single line.
levels.riddle:
A massive diary full of thoughts and dreams,
But hidden inside, a password gleams.
Search through the text with a powerful tool,
Find the word 'SECRET' — don't be a fool.

files.diary.txt:
Day 1: Started working on the server.
Day 2: Installed new security patches.
Day 3: Meeting with the network team.
Day 4: Debugging the authentication module.
Day 5: SECRET_CODE=PHANTOM42 — don't forget this!
Day 6: Routine maintenance.
Day 7: Backup verification complete.
Day 8: Updated firewall rules.
Day 9: Performance tuning finished.
Day 10: Documentation day.

hints: You need to search for a specific word inside a file.
The 'grep' command searches for patterns in files: grep PATTERN FILE

levels.title: Recursive Search
levels.narrative: The password fragments are scattered across multiple files in multiple directories. You need to search through ALL of them at once to find where 'FRAGMENT' appears.
levels.riddle:
Files within files, folders within folders deep,
The word 'FRAGMENT' hides where secrets sleep.
Search through all directories, leave no stone unturned,
A recursive flag is what must be learned.

files.surface.txt: Nothing important here on the surface.

hints: You need to search for text across ALL files in ALL subdirectories.
grep has a recursive flag that searches through all files in subdirectories.

levels.title: The Lost File
levels.narrative: Somewhere deep in the server's directory tree, there's a file called 'golden_key.txt'. You don't know where it is, but you know its name. Time to find it.
levels.riddle:
A golden key is lost somewhere below,
Deep in the tree where few dare to go.
You know its name but not its place —
Use 'find' to locate it in the space.

files.readme.txt: The golden_key.txt is hidden somewhere in the directories below.

hints: You need to find a file by name, but you don't know which directory it's in.
The 'find' command searches for files: find . -name 'filename'

levels.title: By Their Nature
levels.narrative: The server has a mix of files and directories. You need to find all directories that contain the word 'secret' in their name. Only directories — not files!
levels.riddle:
Many files and folders share this space,
But only folders of a 'secret' name embrace.
Find the directories, ignore the files,
Use type and name to search through the aisles.

files.secret_notes.txt: This is a FILE, not a directory!

hints: You need to find only DIRECTORIES (not files) with 'secret' in the name.
The find command can filter by type: -type d for directories, -type f for files.
